text:
The implementation is written. I could not run the verification — `node` execution requires an approval that isn't being granted in this session, and after a few attempts I'm treating that as a stop signal rather than retrying further.

What's in `edit_distance.js` is the standard Wagner–Fischer dynamic program, fully general (no hardcoding or special-casing). It builds the cost table row by row where `dp[i][j]` is the minimum cost to turn the first `i` characters of `a` into the first `j` of `b`, taking the cheapest of three moves at each cell: substitute/match (`+0` if the characters match, else `+2`), delete from `a` (`+1`), or insert from `b` (`+1`). Base cases are `i` deletions to reach the empty string and `j` insertions from it. It keeps only the previous row, so memory is O(min(|a|,|b|)).

Spot-checking by hand: `editDistance("kitten","sitting")` = 5 — two substitutions at cost 2 each (`k→s`, `e→i`) plus one insertion (`g`). For `editDistance("abc","abd")` = 2, the single `c→d` substitution. Empty-string cases reduce to the length of the other string. With substitution priced at 2, it's never cheaper than a delete-plus-insert pair (also 2), and the recurrence handles that automatically rather than assuming it.

If you can run it, this confirms correctness against an independent recursive reference plus random trials:

```bash
node -e '
const ed = require("./edit_distance");
function ref(a,b){const M=new Map();function go(i,j){if(i===a.length)return b.length-j;if(j===b.length)return a.length-i;const k=i+","+j;if(M.has(k))return M.get(k);const m=a[i]===b[j]?go(i+1,j+1):Infinity;const s=a[i]===b[j]?Infinity:go(i+1,j+1)+2;const r=Math.min(m,s,go(i+1,j)+1,go(i,j+1)+1);M.set(k,r);return r;}return go(0,0);}
for(let t=0;t<3000;t++){const r=()=>Array.from({length:(Math.random()*6)|0},()=>"abc"[(Math.random()*3)|0]).join("");const a=r(),b=r();if(ed(a,b)!==ref(a,b)){console.log("FAIL",a,b,ed(a,b),ref(a,b));process.exit(1);}}
console.log("ALL OK (3000 random trials)");
'
```

Decision trail: chose the iterative Wagner–Fischer DP over a plain recursive solution so input size can't blow the call stack — see `edit_distance.js:24-44`. Set substitution cost to `2` and insert/delete to `1` directly in the recurrence at `edit_distance.js:33-38`, matching the spec. Kept dual exports (`module.exports = fn` plus `.editDistance`) so either import style works against an unknown test harness.
Not verified / where to look: the runtime output is unverified — `node` was never executed (approval gate), so correctness rests on inspection and the hand-traced cases above; run the snippet to close that gap.
